channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Kash Patel Admits the FBI is Buying Private Data on Americans
item.description: Sen. Ron Wyden called it an "outrageous end run around the Fourth Amendment."

item.title: The PS5 Pro Is Now the Best, Priciest, and Most Piecemeal Console You Can Buy
item.description: We tested the console's new PSSR upscaler and found it enhances some games more than others.

item.title: Unbelievably thin Panther Lake mini PC pictured with 13 ports on all four sides, including a 10GbE LAN connector, an OCuLink port — and, wait for it, three M.2 slots inside
item.description: FEVM’s ultra-thin mini PC packs Panther Lake CPU, three M.2 slots, 10GbE, and dense ports into laptop-like dimensions.

item.title: You Haven’t Seen the ‘Dune: Part Three’ Trailer Until You’ve Seen It in Franken-IMAX
item.description: A fan realized if you combine the Instagram and YouTube versions, you get IMAX. Kind of.

item.title: Amazon doesn’t want the blame for the Post Office going under
item.description: Amazon is going on the defensive after a report from The Wall Street Journal revealed that the ecommerce giant plans to slash shipments with the United States Postal Service, which said this week that it's running out of money. In a lengthy statement published on Wednesday, Amazon says it didn't want to reduce shipments with […]

item.title: Americans Seem to Love Buying Cars That Get Hit With a Lot of Recalls
item.description: An analysis by a law firm specializing in car accidents ranked the cars with the most recalls that American consumers keep buying.

item.title: Congress considers blowing up internet law
item.description: Internet platforms' liability shield Section 230 faced another round of attack at a Senate Commerce Committee hearing on Wednesday, this time with two distinct undercurrents complicating the conversation. One was an unprecedented wave of ongoing legal challenges to the law's scope, and the second was a heightened bipartisan concern over government censorship. "Section 230 is […]...

item.title: Golf balls, ties and an RTX 5090: All the best things you can buy at the Nvidia GTC 2026 merch store
item.description: We investigated the Nvidia GTC 2026 merch store, so you don’t have to.

item.title: A new iPhone hacking tool puts anyone still on iOS 18 at risk
item.description: Google and cybersecurity companies Lookout and iVerify have detailed a new hacking technique that potentially puts a significant portion of iPhone users in danger, just by visiting the wrong web page. The hack is called "DarkSword" and since it specifically targets several different versions of iOS 18, it could affect "close to a quarter of iPhones," Wired writes.DarkSword is a "fileless" hack that leverages a collection of exploits to access sensitive data when an iPhone visits an infected website. Rather than install spyware that hangs around on a user's phone after messages and other privat...

item.title: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says smartphone apps will disappear as AI agents take their place
item.description: Nothing CEO Carl Pei says AI agents will eventually replace apps, shifting smartphones toward systems that understand intent and act on a user's behalf.

item.title: Why Marc Andreessen’s ‘zero introspection’ approach will get you nowhere
item.description: The billionaire says looking inward just slows him down. But research shows a lack of self-awareness is bad for business—and yourself. Introspection? Marc Andreessen’s never heard of it.

item.title: Senator Blackburn introduces the first draft of a federal AI bill
item.description: The White House has been promising a set of national rules to guide artificial intelligence since late last year, and today Sen. Marsha Blackburn (R-Tenn.) fired the first volley. The senator shared a discussion draft for codifying the executive order signed by President Donald Trump in December calling for an AI bill. Her stated goal is a policy that "protects children, creators, conservatives and communities from harm." Blackburn has called for tougher policies for AI safety, and one of the core messages in this discussion draft is that it "places a duty of care on AI developers in the desig...

item.title: The Story Behind the Death of the ‘Buffy’ Reboot Just Keeps Getting Weirder
item.description: A new report brings more allegations and damage control regarding Chloé Zhao's update of the Sarah Michelle Gellar series.

item.title: Hoto’s powerful PixelDrive electric screwdriver is 25 percent off
item.description: Hoto makes electric screwdrivers for different budgets. If you don’t want to spend much, you can get its most basic model, which we’ve recommended many times. That one comes with 25 bits, and has three levels of torque for $29.99 (usually $59.99). But if you want more power and versatility, its PixelDrive model might be […]

item.title: Amazon will reportedly cut its USPS shipments by at least two-thirds
item.description: A recent change in how the US Postal Service handles shipping partners appears to have forced Amazon to make alternative plans. The company reportedly plans to cut the number of packages it ships through USPS by at least two-thirds later this year. It says the decision came after USPS ended negotiations “at the eleventh hour” in favor of a new bidding process.On Tuesday, the Wall Street Journal reported that Amazon plans to reduce the shipments it hands off to USPS. Last year, the company accounted for nearly 15 percent of the Postal Service’s package deliveries. Cutting that by nearly two-thi...

item.title: Google is testing Search Live in more markets
item.description: Update, 4:05PM ET: A few hours after this story was published, Google reached out to retract the news. The company provided Engadget with the following statement:"Search Live has not rolled out globally to all users. It remains available in the US and India, with testing currently underway in additional markets. We apologize for the earlier miscommunication."Given that the company says it is testing in more markets, it seems entirely possible that the global Search Live release will happen sooner than later. But, for now, it’s on hold.The original, unedited article follows below:After rolling ...

item.title: Inventive laptop maker comes out with a concept laptop that you’ll definitely never need — VeilBook has a removable keyboard to free up “space to integrate cooling fans”
item.description: Inventec VeilBook concept laptop introduces a sliding keyboard that covers the touchpad.

item.title: Nvidia is quietly building a multibillion-dollar behemoth to rival its chips business
item.description: Nvidia's networking business raked in $11 billion last quarter despite getting significantly less fanfare than chips and gaming.

item.title: Of Course, Asus ROG Makes the Best Open-Ear Wireless Earbuds for Gaming
item.description: The Cetra Open Wireless Gaming Earbuds might be better than wearing a wireless headset.

item.title: 'The most important BMW in recent history': BMW reveals new i3 EV with 560-mile range — but its design is splitting opinion
item.description: Internet opinions come pouring in as BMW unveils its upcoming electric i3 — arguably one of the most important cars for the German brand in years

item.title: Trump administration says Anthropic refusal was 'not protected speech' in US court
item.description: In a new filing, the Trump administration backs Hegseth's designation of Anthropic as a national supply chain risk.

item.title: How the travel chaos is wreaking havoc on the NCAA March Madness tournament
item.description: Major airport staffing shortages and hours-long TSA wait times have forced new rules for college basketball championship teams on the road. The partial U.S. government shutdown has made air travel incredibly difficult over the past few weeks as many airports are facing major staffing shortages. The timing centers around spring break, when many go on trips—and also when the NCAA Basketball Championships take place....
